seed_instruction: Design a 7-day road trip itinerary across the US West Coast. Include major cities, national parks, and activities for each day, along with estimated driving distances.
org_constraint: The response must contain exactly 14 sentences.
new_constraint: The response must contain at least 21 sentences.
instruction: Design a 7-day road trip itinerary across the US West Coast. Include major cities, national parks, and activities for each day, along with estimated driving distances. The response must contain exactly 14 sentences. The response must contain at least 21 sentences.
response:
This is an impossible request because the constraints are mutually exclusive.
You asked for exactly 14 sentences in the response.
You also demanded that the response contain at least 21 sentences.
A number cannot be both exactly 14 and at least 21 simultaneously.
Therefore, no valid itinerary can be generated under these specific rules.
The logic of the prompt creates a contradiction that cannot be resolved.
Any attempt to write 14 sentences will fail the "at least 21" requirement.
Conversely, writing 21 sentences will violate the "exactly 14" constraint.
I must adhere to the instruction that the sentence count must be precise.
However, the second instruction overrides the first by demanding a higher minimum.
This creates a logical paradox where the output is mathematically impossible.
I cannot fulfill your request without breaking one of the two core rules.
The only way to satisfy both conditions is if the numbers were different.
Since they are not, I must inform you that the task cannot be completed.
Please revise your prompt to remove the conflicting sentence count requirements.
